model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The checker uses a fixed 256-step budget, and the baseline batch size only exposes the model to a small fraction of the training set. Increasing batch size to 256 should improve data coverage within the same step budget, while a modest learning-rate increase helps preserve optimization progress with the larger batch.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current model is extremely shallow and still spends a large share of its capacity in the fully connected head. Adding one more pooled convolution stage while modestly widening the stem should shift capacity toward better spatial feature extraction without making the overall model excessively large.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The deeper 3-block model helped substantially, which suggests the workload is still capacity-limited. A modest width increase keeps the architecture simple while giving each stage more representational power without changing the training loop or introducing new regularization complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The wider and deeper feature extractor is now feeding a relatively narrow MLP bottleneck. Expanding the hidden layer is a simple way to let the classifier use the richer convolutional features without changing optimization behavior.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The model has become substantially larger and the run still uses a short fixed-step budget. A modest learning-rate increase is a simple way to push faster optimization progress without changing the successful architecture.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The higher learning rate regressed, and this short-budget setting may also be over-regularized now that the model is larger. Restoring the stronger learning rate while slightly reducing weight decay is a simple way to test whether the current architecture wants less regularization to convert its added capacity into lower validation loss.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Recent gains have come from adding capacity, and the last regularization tweak was effectively neutral. A small additional width increase keeps the same successful 3-stage design while testing whether the model is still capacity-limited in this fixed-step regime.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current wider backbone is still followed by a relatively small MLP head compared with the flattened feature size. A moderate hidden-size increase is a simple next step to see if the classifier remains the main bottleneck.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 192","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The strongest recent gain came from enlarging the classifier bottleneck, which suggests the head is still limiting how much of the wider backbone’s representation reaches the logits. Increasing the hidden size again is a direct continuation of the best-performing trend.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 192","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increasing the classifier width has continued to improve validation loss, although more slowly. A further step to 256 keeps following the strongest observed direction while remaining a simple change to the current best architecture.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 192","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The larger classifier overshot, but the fixed-step setup still rewards seeing more examples per optimizer step. Restoring the stronger 192-wide head while modestly increasing batch size tests whether additional data coverage can beat the current best without changing the successful backbone.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The larger batch regressed, so the next worthwhile change is to return to the stronger 256-step geometry and test whether AdamW improves generalization on the current high-capacity model. This keeps the best architecture intact while exploring a different optimizer dynamic.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Switching to AdamW improved validation loss materially, which makes the decoupled weight-decay setting worth retuning. A moderate increase to 1e-4 tests whether slightly stronger regularization improves generalization on the current high-capacity model.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The AdamW switch helped, but slightly stronger weight decay did not. In a short 256-step regime, lowering Adam’s second-moment timescale can improve responsiveness, so restoring the better decay value and reducing beta2 is a targeted optimizer-dynamics test.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"AdamW was the real improvement, while the beta2 reduction regressed. The next high-value refinement is to restore the stronger betas and test a slightly smaller learning rate, which may fit the short-budget AdamW dynamics of this larger model better than 1e-3.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 28","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The lower learning rate regressed, so the useful next move is to restore the stronger AdamW setting and test a modest backbone width increase. Earlier capacity increases helped repeatedly, and AdamW may let a slightly wider model convert that capacity into better validation loss.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 28","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Pushing width beyond 24 regressed, so the productive direction is to return to the better-capacity setting and test a very small amount of dropout. The current AdamW model is fairly large, and mild regularization may improve validation loss without the stronger penalty that hurt when weight decay was increased.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The dropout change regressed sharply, so the final useful attempt is to restore the stronger AdamW baseline and test a very small learning-rate increase around the current optimum. This keeps the best architecture and optimizer while probing whether the improved AdamW setup wants slightly more step size than 1e-3.","secondary_modes":["caching","indexing"]}
